title: Message Level 1: Store Sequence Messages
text: Message Level 1: Store Sequence Messages These messages show whether the entity is being updated or inserted (Store) or deleted (Delete), or whether the named referential integrity constraint is being applied to a many entity. Table 1. Store Sequence Messages for the One Entity Type Message Store Store: Table/Filename dbocc: X occ: Y Delete Delete: Table/Filename dbocc: X occ: Y Table 2. Store Sequence Messages for the Many Entity Type Message NULLIFY Nulwld: ManyTable dbocc: X occ: Y CASCADING Delwld: ManyTable dbocc: X occ: Y RESTRICTED Readal: ManyTable dbocc: X occ: Y The number following dbocc shows the sequence number of the occurrence as it was retrieved from the database. If dbocc is equal to zero, the occurrence was inserted. The number following occ shows the sequence number of the occurrence in the component. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
